hypotheses.name: Dorothea confidence stratification
hypotheses.method: Load Dorothea regulon database (dorothea_human.tsv). Split 1137 TF-target pairs among 209 named genes by confidence tier: A/B (N=266, high) vs D/E (N=775, low). Compute AUROC for high-conf vs background (5000 non-Dorothea/non-STRING pairs) at each of 12 scGPT layers. Permutation control: 500 label shuffles at layer 8.
hypotheses.primary_metric: AUROC high-confidence vs background, all layers
hypotheses.result_value: AUROC=0.671 at L8, range 0.618-0.679 across 12 layers, 12/12 layers significant (p<1e-10), perm_p=0.000 (0/500 shuffles exceed real)
hypotheses.next_action: Combine Dorothea confidence stratification with STRING score stratification in a joint model. Test if Dorothea A/B signal persists after partialling out STRING score (independent regulatory vs PPI signal).

hypotheses.name: TF activation hub centrality
hypotheses.method: From TRRUST (named genes only), count activation targets per TF (N=39 TFs) and repression targets per TF (N=32 TFs). Compute mean L2 distance from each TF to all other named genes as centrality proxy. Spearman(activation_degree, -mean_dist) at each of 12 layers. Also top-15 activation vs top-15 repression TF centrality comparison (Mann-Whitney AUROC).
hypotheses.result_value: Layer 8: Spearman=-0.047 p=0.74. Top-K AUROC=0.527 p=0.41. 0/12 layers significant for either metric.
hypotheses.next_action: Retire. The pairwise activation proximity signal (iter_0023 H03) does not generalize to TF hub centrality. Consider instead testing whether activation targets of a single high-degree TF (e.g., JUN) form a compact cluster.
hypotheses.retirement_reason: First negative - monitor for second negative before retiring

hypotheses.name: GO ontology comparison BP vs MF vs CC
hypotheses.method: Query mygene API for GO BP, MF, CC terms for 209 named genes. Compute pairwise Jaccard similarity for each ontology (21736 pairs). Spearman(Jaccard, -L2_distance) at each of 12 scGPT layers. Compare effect sizes and significance across ontologies.
hypotheses.result_value: L8: CC=0.106(p=9e-56), MF=0.089(p=3e-39), BP=0.081(p=5e-33). All 12/12 layers significant for all three. CC peak=0.124 at L5. BP shows deepening trend L0=0.050 to L7=0.083.
hypotheses.next_action: Use GO CC as primary ontology anchor. Test combined GO CC + STRING score + Dorothea confidence in a multi-predictor regression model for embedding distance. Also investigate which CC terms (subcellular compartments) drive the signal.
